title: UK PM Starmer Rejects Support for US Naval Blockade of Strait of Hormuz
detail: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer publicly confirmed the UK will not support the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, creating a visible public rift among Western allies over maritime escalation strategy in the Iran conflict. The split signals growing divergence between Washington and London over how far to push Iran militarily.

media.caption: UK PM Starmer confirms Britain will not back US Hormuz naval blockade

title: Australia Calls for Hormuz Reopening; Defence Chief Says Ships 'Ready and Capable'
detail: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese called on the US and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to international shipping. Australia's Defence chief stated Australian naval vessels are 'ready and capable' — signaling Canberra's potential involvement in any multilateral naval effort to restore freedom of navigation in the strait.

media.caption: Australian PM Albanese calls for the Strait of Hormuz to be reopened to international shipping

title: BBC Poll: Israelis War-Weary but Majority Still Oppose Iran Ceasefire
detail: A BBC poll finds Israelis increasingly war-weary but a majority still oppose a ceasefire with Iran, complicating diplomatic efforts as US-brokered talks continue in the wake of the Islamabad negotiations collapse. The polling data underscores the domestic political constraints facing Israeli leadership as international pressure for de-escalation mounts.

media.caption: BBC poll shows Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire
